Provide coding agents with the Gemini API developer skill. 

The skill provides agents with the latest SDK knowledge:
 API feature sets
 Current models and SDKs
 Sample code
 Documentation links

Check out the research on how the Gemini API skill was built and evaluated

A 117-prompt evaluation harness across Python and TypeScript measures performance improvement by comparing "vanilla" model outputs against those using the new system instructions and tools. This testing identifies exactly where the skill successfully prevents the use of legacy

The top-performing gemini-3.1-pro-preview model showed strong efficacy across nearly all domains when the skill was enabled. While SDK Usage maintained a 95% pass rate, failures typically stemmed from complex requests or prompts explicitly targeting legacy Gemini 2.0 models.

Two easy optimizations
(2/3)  Take inference to Cloud Run:  Auto-scales during spikes and drops to zero when offline. You only pay for the milliseconds your code runs.

(3/3)  Use Gemini Flash for easy tasks, Pro for heavy lifting: Flash for fast, cheap formatting and extraction. Pro for reasoning. Same product, better margin.

String manipulation developer challenge: find the first non-repeating character. Return the character, or null if they all repeat. Use any language of your choice. The shortest solution wins!
If you are calling count inside a loop, think about what that does to your time complexity.

The short Python version works by scanning left to right and returning the first character whose total count is one. It is concise but not O(n) because the count runs each time. A more efficient approach would build a frequency map in one pass, then scan again to find the first

New in Claude Code: auto mode.

Instead of approving every file write and bash command, or skipping permissions entirely, auto mode lets Claude make permission decisions on your behalf.

Safeguards check each action before it runs.

Before each tool call, a classifier reviews it for potentially destructive actions. Safe actions proceed automatically. Risky ones get blocked, and Claude takes a different approach.

This reduces risk but doesn't eliminate it. We recommend using it in isolated environments.

Available now as a research preview on the Team plan. Enterprise and API access rolling out in the coming days.

Enable with claude --enable-auto-mode, then cycle to it with Shift+Tab.

New from the Anthropic Economic Index: how people’s use of Claude changes with experience.

Longer-term users are more likely to iterate carefully with Claude, and less likely to hand it full autonomy. They attempt higher-value tasks, and receive more successful responses.

We find that since November 2025, consumer use has become less concentrated: the top 10 tasks now make up 19% of conversations, down from 24%. We also see a rise in personal queries, and continued convergence in adoption rates in the US.
